name: Sleeping Guard
description: A city guard who has dozed off at his post. His helmet has slipped over one eye and a half-eaten apple rests in his lap.

start.narrative: The guard blinks and reaches for his sword, then relaxes when he sees you.

apologetic.text: Ah, don't worry about it. Double shift, y'know? Captain's had us all on edge since those lights started up at the ruins.
apologetic.narrative: He rubs his eyes and yawns.

defensive.text: Hey now, I was just... resting my eyes! Nothing gets past me. Well, almost nothing.
defensive.narrative: He straightens up and adjusts his helmet.

gate-info.text: North road leads out to the crossroads and the old farm. Beyond that, the forest — and further still, the Eltharan ruins. I wouldn't go past the crossroads at night if I were you.
gate-info.narrative: He lowers his voice and glances through the gate.

lights.text: Strange green lights, flickering in the ruins after dark. The farmer's boy swears he heard chanting. Priestess says it's old magic stirring. Whatever it is, it's got the captain spooked.
lights.narrative: The guard shivers despite the mild air.
